After that, he and his bots went from one house to the next, killing every human they found .” “ Fuck ,” was all she could manage . “It was a very long time ago, I think. Clearly, his value of humans has not much changed .” “It doesn’t surprise me, not really. Still makes me sick…but everything about him does that. I just…what the hell did those people do to deserve that? What have any of us done ?” “Cheyenne is capable of providing for your every need and allowing you a comfortable life,” Ronin said. He resumed walking, still holding her hand, and she fell into step beside him. “But it will not, so long as he is there .” The humans rallied together to fight for their homes, to fight for whatever was left of their lives. But they couldn’t stand against him. They were slaughtered in the street . Ronin’s words echoed through her mind. People were still being slaughtered, one at a time, for Warlord’s amusement. They lived in constant fear and hopelessness. And here she was, leaving without a word, getting herself to someplace safer . She frowned, guilt heavy in her stomach . “Well…I hope you have some more pleasant stories to tell, if we’re going to be traveling for a while .” Seeming to know where her mind dwelled, Ronin lifted her hand and kissed it. “Perhaps we’ll find ourselves in a comfortable place to camp, soon enough, so I may suitably distract you .” She stared at him, shaking her head. “That is such a man thing to say .” “I can’t tell if I’ve offended you or not .” “That’s another man thing .” “Perhaps I can take some comfort, then, in human males being as clueless about women as bots are .” “We’re not really that hard to figure out,” she said, grinning despite everything . His only answer was an incredulous scoff . Whenever they stopped, she knew the events of the last few days would crash down upon her, but for now she could find some contentment with Ronin . Chapter Twenty- Three T hey walked for hours in the dark, each step harder than the last for Lara. The coat helped with the biting wind, but the temperature dropped further as the night went on, cold seeping through her layers of clothing to chill her skin. She kept her head down to shield her stinging eyes, watching the ground — every inch of it the same dirt, the same grass — slowly creep by . Lara had dealt with cold and wind all her life, but the structures around Cheyenne blocked the worst of it, and even four rickety walls were better than this wide-open nothingness . She pulled up the collar of the coat, clutching it around her neck to seal in some heat. Her fingers were going numb, and her cheeks and nose stung beneath her scarf, which she’d wrapped around her lower face. Her pack sagged; she hopped to kick it up, grasping a strap in one hand to keep it in place . Ronin’s tracks continued through the dirt. She followed them, comforted that the crunching of his boots was only a few feet ahead; lifting her head to check was too much effort, and would only expose her to blasts of stinging dust . “R-R-Ronin!” she called, chin quivering . His steady footfalls stopped, and she risked a glance up to see him turn toward her. His mouth and nose were hidden behind his mask . “What’s wrong ?” “F- Freezing .” “We have to keep moving, Lara. You’re only going to get colder if you stop .” She moved to his side, body shaking. “How m-much farther ?” “A long way.” She heard his frown in his tone. Squinting against the wind, she watched him adjust his packs to shrug out of one sleeve on his coat. He drew Lara into it, wrapping an arm around her shoulders, and his warmth suffused her. She pressed her face to his side and embraced him . Lara’s eyes drifted shut, and she leaned against him. Her legs were stiff and weak after miles of trekking through the Dust. If only she could sleep, just a little … Ronin squeezed her arm. “Come on.” He walked forward, and she forced her legs to move along with him to keep close to his warmth. “This’ll be the hardest night, if we’re lucky .” She swallowed her guilt — he could go all night and through the next day without missing a step, and she was just slowing him down — and focused on walking, on his warmth, on the rustle of fabric as he moved. Anything but how cold and exhausted she was . “Distract me, Ronin .” “What kind of places would you like to see ?” “The ocean,” she replied without hesitation. “I saw a picture in one of the books in the attic.” It had been such a beautiful, vibrant blue . “Pictures don’t do it justice.” He extended his free hand, sweeping it across the entire horizon. “It’s so vast that you’d have to journey for weeks on its waters to reach the other side. Everything is tiny compared to the ocean. Us, all of our worries, needs, problems …” “ Go on .” “All of it is insignificant in the face of something so massive .” “Did you see it? The other side ?” “If I have, I don’t remember. But I know it’s there .” “Is the water really that blue ?” “Sometimes. With shades of green or gray. I think, in some places, it has no color at all. It’s so clear you can see to the bottom .” “I’d like to see them all .” He rubbed his hand up and down
